sources: Academic repositories — political science and public policy

entries.title: British Columbia Carbon Tax (2008)
entries.description: Revenue-neutral carbon tax introduced in British Columbia, Canada. Tax applied to fossil fuels, offset by reductions in other taxes.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Carbon emissions fell 5–15% relative to other Canadian provinces while GDP growth remained comparable. The revenue-neutral design significantly reduced political opposition. Widely cited as a successful model.

entries.title: Taiwan National Health Insurance (1995)
entries.description: Single-payer universal healthcare system covering all residents of Taiwan, replacing a fragmented multi-payer system.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Coverage expanded from ~59% to 99% of population within 2 years. Administrative costs significantly lower than multi-payer alternatives. Consistently ranked among the world's highest-quality healthcare systems by international indices.

entries.title: New Zealand MMP Electoral Reform (1996)
entries.description: Switch from first-past-the-post to mixed-member proportional representation following a citizen-initiated referendum.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Improved proportionality and representation of minority groups in parliament. Coalition government became the norm, increasing negotiation requirements. Overall democratic satisfaction remained high.

hindering: initial voter confusion with new system

entries.title: Finland Basic Income Pilot (2017–2018)
entries.description: Two-year randomised controlled trial of unconditional basic income for 2,000 unemployed adults.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Participants reported improved wellbeing, trust in institutions, and reduced bureaucratic burden. Modest positive employment effects. Full-scale fiscal sustainability unresolved — programme not continued.

hindering: political will did not survive change of government

entries.title: Norway Government Pension Fund Global (1990)
entries.description: Sovereign wealth fund established to manage oil revenues for future generations, governed by an independent central bank unit.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Grew to over $1.7 trillion by 2024, generating significant returns for future generations. Transparent governance and strict fiscal rules prevented Dutch disease effects. Widely cited as the global model for resource revenue management.

hindering: initial political pressure to spend more domestically

entries.title: Hong Kong ICAC Anti-Corruption Commission (1974)
entries.description: Independent Commission Against Corruption established with direct reporting to head of government, operational independence, and broad investigative powers.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Corruption perception indices improved dramatically within a decade. Police corruption nearly eliminated. Model replicated in multiple jurisdictions including Singapore and Botswana.

promoting: full independence from police and government
hindering: initial resistance from existing power structures
required sustained political protection over multiple administrations

entries.title: UK National Living Wage Introduction (2016)
entries.description: Statutory national living wage set above the existing minimum wage, with automatic annual uprating mechanism.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Raised wages for approximately 1.6 million workers with limited evidence of significant employment effects in most sectors. Some negative effects in hospitality and retail in regions with low pre-existing wages.

promoting: automatic uprating mechanism reduced political volatility
hindering: regional variation in labour markets not fully addressed
some automation acceleration in low-wage sectors

entries.title: South African Constitution Bill of Rights (1996)
entries.description: Comprehensive constitutionalisation of socioeconomic rights including housing, healthcare, food, water, and social security, with justiciability provisions.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Constitutional court produced landmark progressive judgments (Grootboom, TAC). Rights created legal leverage for civil society. Implementation constrained by fiscal capacity and institutional weakness. Partial realisation of socioeconomic guarantees.

entries.title: Portugal Drug Decriminalisation (2001)
entries.description: Decriminalisation of personal possession of all drugs, combined with investment in treatment and harm reduction services.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Drug-related HIV infections, overdose deaths, and incarceration fell significantly. Drug use rates remained below EU average. Treatment uptake increased substantially. Widely cited as an evidence-based success.

hindering: international treaty obligations required careful navigation

entries.title: Zimbabwe Fast Track Land Reform (2000)
entries.description: Accelerated land redistribution from commercial farmers to smallholders, conducted without compensation and with limited planning.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Agricultural output collapsed by approximately 50% within 5 years. Food security crisis. Hyperinflation. Significant emigration of skilled workers. Long-term economic damage persisted for decades.

hindering: no transition support for new farmers

entries.title: Finland Education System Reform (1970s–1990s)
entries.description: Comprehensive transformation of the Finnish education system: elimination of tracking, teacher professionalisation, equity-focused funding, and emphasis on play-based early learning.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Finland rose to top-tier international rankings (PISA) with among the lowest variance between high and low performers globally. Achieved without high-stakes testing. Teacher profession became highly selective and respected.

hindering: 30-year timeframe required sustained political will across multiple governments

entries.title: Greek Austerity Programme (2010–2015)
entries.description: IMF/EU-mandated fiscal consolidation programme requiring large reductions in public spending, wages, and pensions in exchange for bailout financing.
entries.outcomeNarrative: GDP fell approximately 25% over five years — comparable to the Great Depression. Unemployment reached 27%. Healthcare system severely damaged. Debt-to-GDP ratio increased despite fiscal adjustment due to GDP collapse. Social outcomes severely deteriorated.

entries.title: NAFTA (North American Free Trade Agreement) (1994)
entries.description: Trilateral trade agreement eliminating tariffs and trade barriers between USA, Canada, and Mexico.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Tripled trade volumes between member countries. Significant GDP gains for Mexico. Displaced manufacturing workers in USA and Canada with limited support for transition. Increased inequality within countries while reducing inequality between them.

hindering: distributional effects not addressed in design

entries.title: Singapore Public Housing Programme (HDB) (1960–present)
entries.description: State-led mass public housing programme providing subsidised homeownership through the Housing Development Board, integrated with CPF retirement savings.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Over 80% of Singapore's population housed in HDB flats by 2000. Rapid elimination of slums and squatter settlements. Strong community integration design. One of the highest homeownership rates in the world.

hindering: ethnic quota system required careful management

entries.title: Germany Energiewende Renewable Energy Transition (2000–present)
entries.description: National energy transition policy mandating shift from nuclear and fossil fuels to renewables through feed-in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, and phase-out commitments.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Renewable share of electricity rose from ~6% to ~46% by 2020. Consumer electricity prices among the highest in Europe. Industrial competitiveness concerns emerged. Grid integration challenges significant. Carbon emissions reduction slower than anticipated due to coal phase-out delays.

hindering: nuclear phase-out accelerated coal use temporarily

entries.title: South Africa Truth and Reconciliation Commission (1996)
entries.description: Constitutionally mandated commission offering conditional amnesty for apartheid-era crimes in exchange for full public disclosure, with victim reparations provisions.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Documented approximately 21,000 victim testimonies. Prevented significant violent conflict during transition. Contributed to national narrative of non-violence and reconciliation. Reparations programme underfunded and delayed. Full accountability for perpetrators incomplete.

promoting: moral authority of Mandela and Tutu

entries.title: Kansas Tax Cut Experiment (2012–2017)
entries.description: Large-scale income and business tax cuts premised on supply-side growth theory — described as 'real live experiment' by the Governor.
entries.outcomeNarrative: State revenue shortfalls required deep cuts to education and public services. GDP growth did not exceed neighbouring states. Credit downgrade. Policies substantially reversed by bipartisan legislature override in 2017.

promoting: clear ideological commitment enabled decisive implementation
hindering: revenue loss not offset by growth
public service cuts generated political backlash

entries.title: Brazil Bolsa Família (2003)
entries.description: Conditional cash transfer programme linking payments to school attendance and healthcare visits for low-income families.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Reached over 13 million families at peak. Significant reduction in extreme poverty and inequality (Gini coefficient). Improved child school attendance and vaccination rates. Low administrative cost relative to impact. Replicated in 60+ countries.

promoting: unified existing programmes reducing admin overhead
hindering: targeting errors included some non-poor and excluded some poor
conditions created administrative burden for poorest

entries.title: Brexit Referendum (2016)
entries.description: UK referendum on membership of the European Union, resulting in a 51.9% vote to leave.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Implementation proved significantly more complex than anticipated. Trade friction with EU increased measurably. GDP growth lagged comparable economies post-implementation. Political instability during negotiation period was considerable. Outcome assessment contested — depends heavily on ideological priors.

promoting: clear democratic mandate provided implementation authority
hindering: complex interdependencies not fully modelled pre-vote
no detailed implementation plan at time of vote

entries.title: Estonia e-Government Digital Infrastructure (2000–present)
entries.description: National digital identity and e-government infrastructure enabling online voting, tax filing, healthcare, and most government services via a unified digital identity layer.
entries.outcomeNarrative: Over 99% of government services available digitally. Digital signatures legally equivalent to physical. Significant administrative cost savings. High public trust. Widely studied as a global model for digital governance infrastructure.

promoting: constitutional data protection from the start
hindering: cybersecurity incidents required significant response investment
digital divide for elderly and rural populations required parallel access
